channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Australian Mother Faces Deportation Due to Passport Mistake
item.description: Ying-Hsi Chou, a South Australian mother, could face deportation to Taiwan due to a mistake on her passport paperwork from 2012. Born in Myanmar and later becoming a Taiwanese citizen, Chou moved to A...

item.title: Health experts warn of lead risks in informal three-legged pots
item.description: Health authorities in South Africa have raised concerns over the potential dangers of using informally manufactured three-legged pots, commonly used for cooking traditional dishes such as potjiekos. T...

item.title: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Advances Cleanup at Seaway Site in Tonawanda, NY
item.description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Buffalo District, continues its environmental restoration efforts under the Formerly Utilized Remedial Action Program (FUSRAP) at the Seaway Site in Tonawanda...

item.title: How Kenya Benefits from Hosting and Playing in the 2026 FIFA Series
item.description: Kenya is set to receive several important gains from hosting and participating in the 2026 FIFA Series, a set of international friendly matches organised by FIFA. The Harambee Starlets women’s team wi...

item.title: Explore the 7 Themed Areas on the New Disney Adventure Cruise
item.description: The Disney Adventure, the latest and largest ship in the Disney Cruise Line fleet, offers passengers the opportunity to explore seven distinct themed areas, each brimming with the magic and storytelli...

item.title: Former University of Manitoba law dean disbarred in Canada and U.K. after misspending more than $600,000
item.description: A former dean of the University of Manitoba’s law faculty, Jonathan Black-Branch, has been disbarred in both Manitoba and the United Kingdom after being found to have misspent more than $600,000 from ...

item.title: Kim Chiu and Paulo Avelino announce first-ever world tour together
item.description: Kim Chiu and Paulo Avelino are set to embark on their first-ever world tour, dubbed 'Love, KimPau'. The famous showbiz duo, known for their charm and great chemistry on-screen, will be visiting variou...

item.title: Punjab Assembly seeks Nobel Peace Prize nominations for Pakistani leaders after successful US-Iran ceasefire mediation
item.description: A resolution has been tabled in the Punjab Assembly by PML-N Chief Whip Rana Muhammad Arshad, proposing that Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, Chief of Defence Forces and Chief of Army Staff Field Marsha...

item.title: Tanzania integrates TRA and TMDA systems to simplify import and export processes
item.description: The Tanzania Medicines and Medical Devices Authority (TMDA) has officially integrated the TANCIS-TRA and RIMS-TMDA systems to improve the efficiency of electronic import and export procedures. Accordi...

item.title: IMF and Sri Lanka finalise staff-level agreement for $700 million support amid energy and fiscal challenges
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has reached a staff-level pact with Sri Lanka that will unlock around $700 million in financing once approved by the IMF’s executive board later this spring. This...

item.title: Raiders aim to recover season in Perth following Savage injury
item.description: The Canberra Raiders are aiming to reverse a challenging start to their 2026 NRL season after opening with a 1-4 record. Xavier Savage will be sidelined for up to eight weeks with a syndesmosis injury...

item.title: President Expands PiCNG Mandate to Include Electric Vehicles
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don give fresh directive make the Presidential Initiative on Compressed Natural Gas (PiCNG) now include electric vehicles (EVs) for Nigeria transport sector. With the new approva...

item.title: Cleanup efforts continue on Highway 2 following semi crash
item.description: The cleanup operation following a semi-truck crash on Highway 2 near Edmonton is expected to last through the morning. The accident caused significant disruption to traffic, and emergency responders h...

item.title: Sandiganbayan denies motions of two defendants in P16-billion Romualdez wealth case
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has upheld its ruling that two defendants in the ₱16-billion ill-gotten wealth case involving Benjamin Romualdez, the late brother of former First Lady Imelda Marcos, have forfeited ...

item.title: Malawi's Former President Chakwera Engages in Diplomatic Discussions in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Former Malawian President Lazarus Chakwera, who currently serves as the Special Envoy of the Commonwealth Secretary-General, has begun his official engagements in Dar es Salaam. On April 9, 2026, he m...

item.title: Cardinal Tagle and Bishop Maralit Appointed to Vatican's Communications Dicastery
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has appointed two prominent Filipino clerics to the Vatican’s Dicastery for Communications, a move highlighting the Philippines’ growing importance in the Church's global outreach. Cardin...

item.title: Toronto Mental Health Facility Treats Transgender Patient Amid Uncertain Identity and Schizophrenia Diagnosis
item.description: A male patient identifying as transgender is being treated at the Women’s General Forensic Unit of Toronto’s Centre for Addiction and Mental Health (CAMH) despite his uncertain identity and lack of cl...

item.title: British Man Faces Court Over Alleged Leadership of Islamist Group in Somalia
item.description: A British man has appeared in court charged with leading a group of fighters linked to a Somalia-based Islamist terror group. The 32-year-old, whose identity is being withheld, is accused of coordinat...

item.title: Tiny Cooladdi Town Put Up for Sale After Last Residents Leave
item.description: Cooladdi, a tiny settlement in Australia with a rich history as a railway hub, is now on the market after its last two residents, Jo Cornel and Carol Yarrow, decided to move closer to family in Brisba...

item.title: CS Wandayi Faces Parliamentary Scrutiny Over Fuel Crisis After Skipping Probe
item.description: The Energy Cabinet Secretary, Opiyo Wandayi, failed to appear before Parliament after being summoned to explain the fuel shortage concerns in the country. Despite the ongoing fuel crisis, where many s...
